<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Nunaki-iri-hime]][[ja:渟名城入姫命]][[zh:渟名城入姬命]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Nunaki-iri-hime]][[ja:渟名城入姫命]][[zh:渟名城入姬命]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Nunaki-iri-hime]][[ja:渟名城入姫命]][[zh:渟名城入姬命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|WD=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083|1=Yamatohiko|2=ja|3=倭彦命|lt=Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824|1=Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851|1=Princess Kunikatahime}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619|1=Princess Toochiniirihime}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414|1=Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048|1=Yasakairi-hime}}}}





{{Expand Japanese|渟名城入姫命}}
{{Infobox royalty
| house        = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial House of Japan|qq=}}|father       = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}
| successor    = {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|市磯長尾市|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}}
|image=File:Ohyamato-jinja Haiden.jpg|caption=She was the first priestess of [[Ōyamato Shrine]] but was replaced}}

{{ill|Nunaki-iri-hime|ja|渟名城入姫命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|qq=}} (渟名城入姫命) was a {{ill|Japanese|en|Japanese|WD=Q346080|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese}} {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|WD=Q863048|lt=Princess|1=Princess}}.<ref name="aston150–164">{{cite book |author=Aston, William George. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 2 |date=1896 |publisher=The Japan Society London |isbn=9780524053478 |pages=150–164 |author-link=William George Aston}}</ref>

== Overview ==
The main source for the biography of {{ill|Nunaki-iri-hime|ja|渟名城入姫命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|qq=}} is the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]].<ref name="Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.. [[Wikisource]]-1896">{{cite wikisource|year=1896|plaintitle=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|wslink=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697|wspages=151-154|chapter=Book I|publisher=Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.|author-first=William George|author-last=Aston|draft}}</ref> Similar events also covered by the ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogo Shūi|qq=}}''.<ref name="Meiji Japan Society-1925">{{cite book|title=Kogoshūi: Gleanings from Ancient Stories. Translated with an introduction and notes |date=1925 |publisher=Meiji Japan Society |pages=29–30 |translator1-last=Katō |translator1-first=Genchi |translator2-last=Hoshino |translator2-first=Hikoshirō|qq=draft}}</ref>

The narrative relates that pestilence struck during the 5th year of Sujin's rule, and ultimately killed half of the Japanese population. Peasants abandoned their fields in the following year, and rebellion became rampant after eventual events Sujin placed his daughter {{Nihongo|{{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}|豊鍬入姫命}} in charge of the new shrine, where she would become the first {{ill|Saiō|lt=Ise Saiō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiō|qq=}}, .<ref name="aston150–164" />.<ref name="aston150–164" />{{efn|During the reign of Sujin's successor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|Suinin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}}, custody of the sacred treasures were transferred from Toyosukiirihime to Suinin's daughter {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|lt=Yamatohime|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}, who took them first to "Sasahata in Uda" to the east of Miwa. Heading north to {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}, she then traveled eastwards to {{ill|Mino Province|Mino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}, then south to {{ill|Ise Province|Ise|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Province|qq=}}, where she received a revelation from Amaterasu:

{{quotation|Now Ama-terasu no Oho-kami instructed Yamato-hime no Mikoto, saying:—"The province of Ise, of the divine wind, is the land whither repair the waves from the eternal world, the successive waves. It is a secluded and pleasant land. In this land I wish to dwell." In compliance, therefore, with the instruction of the Great Goddess, a shrine was erected to her in the province of Ise. Accordingly an {{ill|Saikū|lt=Abstinence Palace|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} was built at Kaha-kami in Isuzu. This was called the palace of Iso. It was there that Ama-terasu no Oho-kami first descended from Heaven.<ref>{{cite wikisource |author-first= William George |author-last= Aston |chapter= Book I |wslink= Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |plaintitle= Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697 |year= 1896 |publisher= Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.|wspages=176}}</ref>}}

This account serves as the origin myth of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|Grand Shrine of Ise|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}, Amaterasu's chief place of worship.}} <ref>https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8608</ref>.<ref name="Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.. [[Wikisource]]-1896" /><ref name="Meiji Japan Society-1925" /> Meanwhile, {{ill|Yamato Okunitama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama|qq=}} was entrusted to another daughter named {{ill|Nunaki-iri-hime|ja|渟名城入姫命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|qq=}}, but shortly afterwards, her health began to fail. It is recorded that she became [[:en:Emaciated|emaciated]] and lost all of her {{ill|Hair|en|Hair|WD=Q28472|lt=hair|1=Hair}}, which rendered her unable to perform her duties.<ref name="aston150–164" /> These efforts still did not alleviate the ongoing plague, so Sujin decreed that a {{ill|divination|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divination|qq=}} be performed sometime during the 7th year of his reign, that would involve him making a trip to the plain of Kami-asaji, and invoking the {{ill|Yaoyorozu no Kami|lt=eighty myriad deities|simple|Yaoyorozu no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaoyorozu no Kami|qq=}}.<ref name="aston150–164" /> This mode of worship is seen as being potentially linked to a social order of greater complexity, and the organization of the deities of the many clans across the region.<ref>{{Citation |title=Contents |date=2017-12-31 |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1515/9780824862848-001 |work=Himiko and Japan's Elusive Chiefdom of Yamatai |pages=189-191 |access-date=2023-10-24 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6284-8}}</ref>

After these events, Sujin's aunt {{Nihongo|{{ill|Princess Yamato Totohi Momoso|lt=Yamatototohimomoso-hime|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Yamato Totohi Momoso|qq=}}|倭迹迹日百襲媛命}}, the daughter of the seventh emperor {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|Kōrei|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kōrei|qq=}}, acted as a {{ill|Miko|shrine maiden|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miko|qq=}}, and was possessed by a god who identified himself as {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}},<ref name="aston150–164" /><ref name="Ellwood-1990">{{Cite journal|last=Ellwood |first=Robert S. |date=1990 |title=The Sujin Religious Revolution |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/30234018 |journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies |volume=17 |issue=2/3 |pages=199–217 |issn=0304-1042|qq=draft}}</ref> possibly the same entity as Yamato Okunitama. {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|市磯長尾市|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would conduct the rites pertaining to Okunitama, replacing the emaciated {{ill|Nunaki-iri-hime|ja|渟名城入姫命|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nunaki-iri-hime|qq=}}.<ref name="Ellwood-1990" /> {{ill|Ichishi no Nagaochi|ja|市磯長尾市|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|8=fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichishi no Nagaochi|qq=}} would be the ancestor of the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Page:Nihongi by Aston.djvu/208 - Wikisource, the free online library |url=https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Page:Nihongi_by_Aston.djvu/208 |access-date=2023-10-24 |website=en.wikisource.org |language=en}}</ref> This replacement is taken as a shift towards a more patriarchal religion.<ref name="Ellwood-1990" />

==Genealogy==
{{Generations of Jimmu}}

==Notelist==
{{notelist}}

==References==
{{reflist}}

{{Miwa and Yamato Faith}}

{{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}}
<!--removing this template because now we do not need it. All everybodywiki edit histories have been imported-->

{{citation needed|ja|豊鍬入姫命|version=93652464|comment=Imported full ja history}}

 

}}


{{everybodywiki link|Nunaki-iri-hime}}

{{everybodywiki link|Nunaki-iri-hime|Nunaki-iri-hime}}

[[simple:User:Nunaki-iri-hime]][[ja:渟名城入姫命]]

[[ja:豊鍬入姫命]]
[[fr:Nunaki-iri-hime]]
[[ja:渟名城入姫命]]

[[fr:User:Nunaki-iri-hime]]

<!--all this actually mandates is a completion tier 7 ranking. We are not using the promising draft template here. If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Nunaki-iri-hime--><!--Nunaki-iri-hime--><!--Nunaki-iri-hime--><!--Nunaki-iri-hime-->
{{translated page|ja|渟名城入姫命|version=100048402|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--standardizing to enwiki standards where this would merely be the intersection of two categories-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[zh:渟名城入姬命]]
{{wikidata link|Q11561867}}
[[Category:Female Imperial Family Members before the Kofun Period]]
[[Category:Japanese princesses]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
[[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]]
[[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
